meaning: to wake up; to get up

expressions.english: to stand up suddenly; to get up suddenly; to spring up (out of one's seat)
expressions.notes: 벌떡 is an adverb that describes how someone gets up or stands up suddenly and abruptly.

expressions.english: to wake up early
to leave early (before everyone else leaves)
to wake up in the morning
to get up from one's seat
expressions.notes: 자리 = space, spot, seat, position; occasion

expressions.english: Please wake up.; Please get up.; Please stand up.
Are you awake yet?
I'm leaving now (earlier than others).
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄹ 것이다 = will; to be going to + V

expressions.english: Why did you wake up so early?
expressions.notes: 왜 = why; for what reason; how come

expressions.english: I just woke up, so I am still sleepy.
expressions.notes:
방금 = just (now); a moment ago
아직도 = still, (not) yet

expressions.english: What time did you wake up this morning?
I woke up a bit earlier than usual today.

expressions.korean: 내일 일찍 일어나야 하니까 일찍 자요.
expressions.english: You have to wake up early tomorrow, so you should go to sleep early.
expressions.notes:
-이/이/어야 하다 = should; have to; must
-(으)니까 = since, because

expressions.korean: 자는 아침에 일어나는 게 정말 힘들어요.
expressions.english: It is really hard for me to wake up in the morning.
expressions.notes:
정말 = really; very
힘들다 = to be difficult, hard, tough; to have a hard time; to be tiring; to be tired

expressions.korean: 자는 아침에 일어나자마자 운동을 하러 가요.
expressions.english: I go exercise as soon as I wake up in the morning.
expressions.notes:
-자마자 = as soon as + S + V
운동 = exercise; sports
-(으)러 가다 = to go to + V

expressions.korean: 오늘 아침에는 일찍 일어나서 운동을 했어요.
expressions.english: This morning, I woke up early and exercised.

expressions.korean: 공연이 끝나고 관객들이 모두 일어나서 박수를 쳤어요.
expressions.english: After the performance, the whole audience stood up and applauded.
expressions.notes:
공연 = performance, concert
끝나다 = to end; to be over
관객 = audience
-들 is a suffix used to indicate plural.
모두 = all; every; everyone
박수를 치다 = to applaud, clap

expressions.korean: 바닥 청소를 해야 하니까 모두 의자에서 일어나 주세요.
expressions.english: We have to clean the floor, so all (of you) please get up from your chairs.
expressions.notes:
바닥 = floor
청소 = cleaning
청소를 하다 = to clean
의자 = chair
-이/이/여 주세요 = (polite) Please do + something + for me

expressions.korean: 제가 지금 이름을 부르는 사람은 자리에서 일어나 주세요.
expressions.english: The people whose names I call now, please get up from your seats.
expressions.notes:
제 = (polite) my; I
지금 = now
이름 = name
이름을 부르다 = to call one's name

expressions.korean: 어제 술을 많이 마셨더니 아침에 일어나기가 정말 힘들었어요.
expressions.english: It was really hard to get up in the morning because I drank a lot yesterday.
expressions.notes:
술 = alcohol
마시다 = to drink
-았/았더니 = since/because + I/we + V-ed
